segments.text: The word came to Jeremiah from the Lord when King Zedekiah sent to him Pashhur son of Malkijah and the priest Zephaniah son of Maaseiah.
Inquire now of the Lord for us because Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon is attacking us. Perhaps the Lord will perform wonders for us as in times past so that he will withdraw from us.
segments.speaker: Pashhur and Zephaniah

segments.text: I am about to turn against you the weapons of war that are in your hands, which you are using to fight the king of Babylon and the Babylonians who are outside the wall besieging you. And I will gather them inside this city.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: I myself will fight against you with an outstretched hand and a mighty arm in furious anger and in great wrath.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: I will strike down those who live in this city—both man and beast—and they will die of a terrible plague.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: After that, I will give Zedekiah king of Judah, his officials and the people in this city who survive the plague, sword and famine, into the hands of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon and to their enemies who want to kill them. He will put them to the sword; he will show them no mercy or pity or compassion.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: See, I am setting before you the way of life and the way of death.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Whoever stays in this city will die by the sword, famine or plague. But whoever goes out and surrenders to the Babylonians who are besieging you will live; they will escape with their lives.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: I have determined to do this city harm and not good. It will be given into the hands of the king of Babylon, and he will destroy it with fire.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Moreover, say to the royal house of Judah,
Hear the word of the Lord.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: This is what the Lord says to you, house of David: Administer justice every morning; rescue from the hand of the oppressor the one who has been robbed, or my wrath will break out and burn like fire because of the evil you have done— burn with no one to quench it.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: I am against you, Jerusalem, you who live above this valley on the rocky plateau. You who say, 'Who can come against us? Who can enter our refuge?'
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: I will punish you as your deeds deserve. I will kindle a fire in your forests that will consume everything around you.
segments.speaker: The Lord
